perturbation.original_text: Volunteer acknowledges and agrees that all Confidential Information and similar items whether maintained in hard copy, electronically or on -line relating to CCPDT’s business shall remain exclusively the property of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and shall only be used by Volunteer for the purpose(s) permitted by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteer acknowledges and agrees that certain Confidential Information and similar items whether maintained in hard copy, electronically or on -line relating to CCPDT’s business shall remain exclusively the property of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and shall only be used by Volunteer for the purpose(s) permitted by the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'certain' before Confidential Information introduces ambiguity. It's now unclear which specific confidential information is covered by the agreement, contradicting the prior definition. This change introduces uncertainty as to which confidential information falls under this agreement creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteer further agrees that upon termination of service with CCPDT, Volunteer shall return within ten (10) business days all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information of CCPDT and all copies, electronic or otherwise, thereof relating in any way to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers’ business, or in any way obtained during the course of service. Volunteer further agrees that no copies, electronic or otherwise, notes or abstracts of the foregoing shall be retained and that all information will be returned not destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The original text referred to 'all Confidential Information,' implying a comprehensive set of information covered by the agreement. The changed text introduces 'certain Confidential Information,' creating ambiguity about which specific confidential information is subject to the agreement. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which requires the return of 'all documents, files, electronic or otherwise, and property comprising Confidential Information,' because it is now unclear what constitutes 'Confidential Information' to be returned, therefore, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteers shall avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Certification program. Conflicting interests may include but are not limited to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional r elationships.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteers may, at their discretion, avoid personal and professional conflicts of interest in all matters pertaining to the Certification program. Conflicting interests may include but are not limited to such areas as financial, personal relationships, and/or professional relationships.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may, at their discretion' makes avoiding conflicts of interest optional. This directly contradicts the intended purpose of the conflict of interest policy and weakens the ethical obligations of volunteers. The volunteer now has the discretion to avoid conflicting matters, but is at odds with the ethical standing and volunteer code of conduct, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteer Code of Conduct Volunteers shall act fai rly and be impartial and unbiased in their service to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers and to the population of Certificants and candidates. Volunteers shall conduct themselves in a professional manner during the conduct of certific ation business and shall treat all stakeholders with courtesy and respect. Volunteers shall not speak or act on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers without proper written authorization from the CCPDT.
perturbation.justification: The original text states volunteers 'shall avoid' conflicts of interest, creating a mandatory obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'may, at their discretion, avoid' conflicts of interest, making it optional. The contradicted text states 'Volunteers shall act fairly and be impartial and unbiased', indicating a mandatory obligation for fair and unbiased service. The change from 'shall' to 'may, at their discretion' directly contradicts the volunteer code of conduct's mandatory language regarding impartiality and fairness, as a volunteer now has the discretion to engage in conflicts of interest, directly conflicting with their mandatory obligations to be fair, impartial, and unbiased. This directly creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Volunteers shall conduct themselves in a professional manner during the conduct of certific ation business and shall treat all stakeholders with courtesy and respect. Volunteers shall not speak or act on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers without proper written authorization from the CCPDT.
perturbation.changed_text: Volunteers should aim to conduct themselves in a professional manner during the conduct of certification business and aim to treat all stakeholders with courtesy and respect. Volunteers shall not speak or act on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers without proper written authorization from the CCPDT.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'should aim to' makes the professional conduct and respectful treatment of stakeholders a suggestion rather than a requirement. This weakens the expected standard of behavior for volunteers and creates conflict with the intent of maintaining a professional certification program. The obligation to be professional is downgraded to a goal creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time. The provisions of this Agreement and Volunteer’s obligations hereunder shall survive any expiration, termination, or rescission of this Agreement and remain even aft er Volunteer’s relationship with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers ends.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated volunteers 'shall' conduct themselves professionally, making it a requirement. The changed text uses 'should aim to,' weakening the obligation to a mere suggestion. The contradicted text states that violation of the agreement is grounds for termination. By weakening the requirement for professional conduct, it creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether a lack of professional conduct (now only a suggested aim) constitutes a violation that warrants termination. The explanation states this weakens the standard of behavior creating conflict. Thus there is a strong, direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plu s any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: Violation of this Agreement may be grounds for termination of service with the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. A Committee Chair plus any two members of the Executive Committee may elect to remove a volunteer at their discretion, but are not obligated.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will be' to 'may be' makes termination for violating the agreement discretionary. Additionally, adding 'at their discretion, but are not obligated' weakens the authority to remove volunteers. This creates uncertainty about the consequences of breaching the agreement, it introduces uncertainty and contradicts the entire purpose of the agreement creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Volunteers shall participate in the activities required of them to fulfill their charge(s) and serve the mission of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers. Volunteers shall be an advocate for the CCPDT’s certification programs and support its mission. Volunteers shall acknowledge that failure to uphold and abide by the Volunteer Code of Conduct may result in removal from participation in the certification program and volunteers shall willingly accept their termination as discussed in the next section.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Violation of this Agreement will be grounds for termination', which is a definitive consequence. The changed text weakens this by stating 'Violation of this Agreement may be grounds for termination' and adds 'at their discretion, but are not obligated'. This introduces uncertainty about the consequences of violating the agreement. The contradicted text states 'Volunteers shall acknowledge that failure to uphold and abide by the Volunteer Code of Conduct may result in removal...and volunteers shall willingly accept their termination'. The change creates a contradiction because the 'will' implies a mandatory action that volunteers must accept, whereas 'may' and 'at their discretion' introduces optionality, undermining the original intent of mandatory compliance and acceptance of termination. Therefore the 'may' and 'at their discretion' contradict the obligation implied in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon Volunteer and Volunteer’s personal representatives and successors in interest, and shall inure to the benefit of the CCPDT, its successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon Volunteer and Volunteer’s personal representatives and successors in interest, unless otherwise agreed upon, and shall inure to the benefit of the CCPDT, its successors and assigns.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless otherwise agreed upon' introduces a loophole, allowing the volunteer or their representatives to potentially circumvent the agreement's obligations. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the agreement is intended to be a firm commitment, but this addition makes it conditional. There now exists an out for the agreement which is suppose to hold strong, in all situations, creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non Disclosure Agreement Purpose The purpose of this policy is to ensure that those who volunteer their time in service to the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers agree to maintain in confidence anything they learn about the CCPDT’s e xaminations, processes or procedures. Policy Annually the volunteers of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers shall sign the following agreement: In consideration for receiving certain confidential information in connection with provid ing services to or on behalf of the Certification Council for Professional Dog Trainers (CCPDT), I, herein referred to as “Volunteer”, hereby agree and acknowledge:
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a binding agreement on the volunteer and their representatives. The changed text introduces 'unless otherwise agreed upon', creating a loophole where the agreement's obligations can be circumvented. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes the importance of maintaining confidentiality regarding CCPDT's examinations, processes, or procedures and the annual signing of an agreement to that effect. The addition in the changed text undermines the binding nature of the NDA, leading to an in-text contradiction.
